page: Microsoft Azure DC8es_v5 is an x86_64 architecture server instance belonging to the DCEV5 family. It features 8 dedicated vCPUs powered by an Intel processor and runs on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. The instance is configured with 32.0 GB of system memory, providing a ratio of 4.0 GB of RAM per core.

This virtual machine does not include any local storage, meaning all storage requirements must be met using external premium storage options, for which the instance is capable. There are no integrated GPUs, and the configuration includes zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the dedicated CPU allocation and memory-optimized classification of the DCEV5 family provide a stable resource density for workloads requiring consistent compute and memory access. Given the 8 vCPUs and 32.0 GB of RAM, this instance is configured for workloads such as database hosting, memory-intensive applications, and general-purpose enterprise workloads that utilize remote premium storage.
description: Microsoft Azure DC8es_v5 is an x86_64 architecture virtual machine within the DCEV5 server family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It is equipped with 8 dedicated vCPUs powered by an Intel processor and 32.0 GB of system memory, yielding 4.0 GB of RAM per core. This instance does not feature any local storage or integrated GPUs, requiring reliance on external premium storage solutions for data persistence. It also includes zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses. Positioned within the memory-optimized category, the DC8es_v5 offers a stable resource profile for workloads that demand dedicated CPU allocation and consistent memory access. It is suitable for database management, enterprise applications, and memory-intensive processing tasks that do not require local disk space or hardware acceleration.
